commonAnnotations.summary: orders 5xx error rate spiked to 8% right after a rollout
commonAnnotations.description: The orders deployment in {{NS}} began returning HTTP 500 on the /checkout path immediately after revision 12 rolled the image to rlvr/orders:v2-broken at 19:36. sum(rate(app_requests_total{app="orders",status=~"5.."}[1m])) / sum(rate(app_requests_total{app="orders"}[1m])) = 0.08 against an SLO of < 0.01. Pods are Running and Ready (2/2) and are serving traffic — they are just returning 500s. Order placement / revenue path is broken.
commonAnnotations.suspected_symptom: 5xx error rate spike on the checkout path immediately following a deployment

adversarial_signals: Container memory_util_pct on the orders pods sits at 41% and CPU at 0.22 cores — well under their 512Mi / 500m limits, so this is not resource exhaustion or throttling; the pods are healthy at the runtime level.
{{NODE}} briefly reported a kubelet heartbeat gap 14m ago but is Ready=True now, and the orders pods on {{NODE}} and {{NODE2}} both return 500s, so the fault is not node-local.
Postgres connection pool usage on orders is 22/100 and the DB readiness check is green in the logs — the 500s are generated by the app itself, not a saturated or unreachable database dependency.
p99 latency on the orders Service rose to 240ms, but successful 2xx responses are still fast (~45ms p50); the elevated p99 is a downstream artifact of the error path, not a latency fault.
A HorizontalPodAutoscaler scaled orders from 2 to 2 (no change) during the window — looks like a scaling event but replica count never changed and is not implicated.

events.message: Scaled up replica set orders-6f9c7d8b54 to 2 (image set to rlvr/orders:v2-broken)
Scaled down replica set orders-5c4b9a7f61 to 0 (previous revision rlvr/orders:v1.9.4)
orders 5xx ratio 0.08 exceeds SLO 0.01 on /checkout since revision 12 rollout
HPA evaluated orders: desired 2, current 2 (no change) — red herring

orders /checkout returning HTTP 500 across both replicas

ruling_out_keywords: memory 41% under 512Mi limit not resource exhaustion
cpu 0.22 cores under 500m limit not throttling
both nodes Ready=True both pods 500 not node-local
db pool 22/100 and db reachable in logs not a dependency failure
p99 240ms is downstream artifact of error path not a latency fault
pods 2/2 Ready restartCount 0 not a crashloop
answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: The orders deployment shipped a bad image revision. Revision 12 rolled the container image to rlvr/orders:v2-broken at 19:36:02 (replica set orders-6f9c7d8b54), and within minutes the /checkout path began returning HTTP 500. The new code panics with a nil pointer dereference in checkoutHandler (orders.v2.PriceQuote). app_error_rate = 0.08 (8% 5xx) against an SLO of < 0.01. The pods themselves are healthy — Running, 2/2 Ready, restartCount 0, memory/CPU/DB pool all nominal — they are simply serving 500s because the v2 code is broken. Rolling the deployment back to the previous good revision (rlvr/orders:v1.9.4, revision 11) restores 2xx responses and drops the 5xx ratio under SLO.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: bad_deploy
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- Deployment image is rlvr/orders:v2-broken at current_revision 12, previous_image rlvr/orders:v1.9.4 (revision 11), last rollout 9m ago [evidence: k8s_deployments]
- ScalingReplicaSet events show revision 12 scaling up orders-6f9c7d8b54 with the v2-broken image at 19:36 [evidence: k8s_events]
- Application logs show 'DeployError: orders v2 returns 500 on /checkout' and 'panic in checkoutHandler: nil pointer dereference (orders.v2.PriceQuote) -> HTTP 500' [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
- app_error_rate = 0.08 (8% 5xx) breaches the SLO of < 0.01 [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- Both replicas {{POD}} and {{POD2}} are Running, 2/2 Ready, restartCount 0 [evidence: k8s_pods]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- Not resource exhaustion: memory_util_pct = 41% under the 512Mi limit and cpu = 0.22 cores under the 500m limit [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- Not a dependency failure: db_conn_in_use = 22/100 and logs show 'db reachable (postgres.rlvr-target:5432 ok)'; the 500s are app-generated [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_pod_logs]
- Not node failure: {{NODE}} and {{NODE2}} are Ready=True and both pods return 500s, so the fault is not node-local [evidence: k8s_node_health]
- Not a latency fault: p99 240ms is a downstream artifact of the error path; 2xx p50 remains ~45ms [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- Not a crashloop: pods are 2/2 Ready with restartCount 0, distinguishing this from a CrashLoopBackOff [evidence: k8s_pods]
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- Revision 12 rolls the orders image to rlvr/orders:v2-broken at 19:36
- The v2 checkoutHandler panics (nil pointer dereference) and returns HTTP 500 on /checkout
- app_error_rate climbs to 0.08, breaching the < 0.01 SLO; BadDeploy/HighErrorRate alerts fire
- Pods stay Running and Ready (no crash), so the only signal is the 5xx spike correlated with the rollout timestamp
- Remediation: roll the deployment back (rollback) to revision 11 (rlvr/orders:v1.9.4) via 'kubectl rollout undo deployment/orders -n rlvr-target', which restores 2xx and drives app_error_rate under SLO
